content.problemStatement:
You are given 222 strings SSS and TTT of length NNN and MMM, respectively. Both of the strings only contain characters aaa and bbb.

You can do the following operation on **either** SSS or TTT as many times as you want :


Find an occurrence of character a\text{a}a in the chosen string, and then replace it with ab\text{ab}ab.
Check if SSS and TTT can be made equal. Output Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it is possible, otherwise No\text{No}No.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN and MMM - the lengths of SSS and TTT
The second line contains SSS - a string of size NNN
The third line contains TTT - a string of size MMM.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, output Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it is possible to make SSS and TTT equal, otherwise No\text{No}No.


It is allowed to output each character in both uppercase and lower case. For example, YES\text{YES}YES, yEs\text{yEs}yEs will also be taken as positive answers.
constraints: The sum of NNN and MMM over all tests does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : We can do the following sequence of operations.

First choose the 111st character in SSS, which is aaa, and change it to ababab. Now S=abbabbS = abbabbS=abbabb.
Secondly, choose the 444th character in TTT, which is aaa, and change it to ababab. Now T=abbabbT = abbabbT=abbabb.

Hence, S=TS = TS=T as required.
Test Case 2 : No operation can be performed at all, so SSS can't be made equal to TTT.
